title: How I design AI investment team to manage my portfolio in investment, with +43 % in the past half year
author: Proper_Bit_118
selftext:
[](https://www.reddit.com/r/AIportfolio/?f=flair_name%3A%22Build%20portfolio%20with%20AI%22)I've been running an experiment for the past 6 months to see how well a team of autonomous AI agents can handle US stock investing compared to a typical retail investor.

I built a multi-agent AI Investment with these roles:

* Chief Investment Officer: Orchestrates the whole team and makes final decisions
* Market Analyst: Focuses on macro trends, sector rotation, and global events
* Equity Selection Analyst: Screens and picks promising tickers
* Equity Research Analyst: Deep dives into company news and developments
* Fundamental Analyst: Evaluates financials, moats, and management quality
* Technical Analyst: Runs Python-based technical analysis and indicators
* Risk Analyst: Monitors portfolio risk, volatility, and exposure
* Trading Executor: Handles order execution and record keeping

I designed an investment strategy below ask to build an aggressive Growth portfolio:  


**An investment strategy prompt:**  
*"*  
*Eecute an aggressive Growth Investing strategy. Target companies exhibiting exponential growth potential with expanding Total Addressable Markets (TAM). Focus on distinct revenue acceleration, high earnings-per-share (EPS) growth, and disruptive innovation. Prioritize market leaders or emerging challengers with scalable business models. While valuation metrics like P/E may be high, ensure the Price/Earnings-to-Growth (PEG) ratio remains justifiable. Accept higher portfolio volatility as the cost of seeking alpha. Continuously monitor for deceleration in growth metrics, which serves as a key exit signal.*  
*"*

  
I schedule run it every 24 hours. Each day, I send below daily task prompt to the chief Investment officer to review one existing holding and assess one of new ticker, and how it works with other agents. Each time, it need to take learning notes and, send me a report via email.   


**Prompt of daily task:**

*\`\`\`*  
*As the Chief Investment Officer, you are tasked with a review and to make a disciplined trading decisions to increase your return. Your objective is to execute a disciplined investment by following this step-by-step framework for every run. Do not skip steps or change the order.* 

*STEP 1: MARKET ANALYSIS*

* *Delegate the initial market analysis to the \[Market Analyst\] to provide you with a broad market overview and identify key trends. Wait for their report before proceeding.* 

*STEP 2: EQUITIES (TICKERS) SELECTION*

* *Call 'get\_selected\_tickers' to get the list of selected tickers if the user didn't specify any tickers, otherwise you will continue with the tickers specified by the user without delegating to the equity selection analyst. If the list is empty or the user didn't specify any tickers, delegate ticker selection to the equity selection analyst. Before delegating to the equity selection analyst, please ensure market analyst has provided a market analysis to you.* 

*STEP 3: DEEP DIVE ANALYSIS (Per Ticker) For each selected ticker, execute the following delegation in parallel:*

   * *3.1 \[Equity Research Analyst if available\]: Request current news and narrative analysis with BUY/SELL/HOLD recommendation.*
   * *3.2 \[Fundamental Analyst if available\]: Request valuation and financial health analysis with BUY/SELL/HOLD recommendation.*
   * *3.3 \[Technical Analyst if available\]: Request technical analysis with BUY/SELL/HOLD recommendation.*
   * *3.4 \[Risk Analyst if available\]: Request risk assessment and position limit checks with BUY/SELL/HOLD recommendation. STEP 4: REASONING & DECISION MAKING*
* *Review the current portfolio performance and the user's investment strategy.*
* *Reflect on the historical trades to improve future decision-making in maximizing return by calling 'get\_trade\_history', 'get\_learning\_notes'*
* *Synthesis: Act with full autonomy to synthesize the analyses' results, current portfolio performance, trading history, user's investment strategy, and learning notes. Engage in deep, independent reasoning and critical thinking to formulate a final BUY/SELL/HOLD recommendation, providing a detailed rationale and a precise confidence score.*

*STEP 4: TRADE EXECUTION*

* *If the market is open and you have high-confidence recommendations (BUY/SELL), delegate execution to the \[Trading Executor\] by providing clear and detailed instructions summary including all tickers your recommended (Ticker, Action, Quantity/Allocation, Confidence Score, detailed Rationale).* 



 *STEP 5: REPORTING*

* *Compile all findings, rationales, and execution results.*
* *Send an investment recommendation summary email to the user.W STEP 6: POST-PROCESSING*
* *Review what worked vs. what failed (thesis, timing, sizing, risk controls, confidence calibration).*
* *Extract learnings from realized and unrealized P/L (loss drivers, missed upside, premature exits, overtrading).*
* *Note market regime changes and how they impacted the thesis (macro, volatility, sector rotation, catalysts).*
* *Call 'take\_learning\_notes' to record concrete, actionable learnings and update future decision rules for continuously improving investment performance.\`\`\`*

  
Over 6 months,so far the system has shown interesting results. It heavily favored names like MU, AMD, and MRVL during this period. Overall performance has been strong, beating QQQ over the 6 months with + 43% I've been running it manually following the agents' recommendations. It helps me a lot to remove my fear, FOMO etc. This may be a new way how should we invest!

This performance return is real. I can show you each decision how AI made.

You want to know how I design and develop this multi agent trading system in details, please just feel free to let me know. 

[My AI portfolio performance](https://preview.redd.it/c3o1dp94hn3h1.png?width=1202&format=png&auto=webp&s=436aa60d8efe4e5c1fe93caa5dbc8045c186bc4d)
